channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Museum of Illusions Opens at Gateway Theatre of Shopping in Durban
item.description: Durban is set to become home to one of the world’s most unique attractions, the Museum of Illusions, which will officially open at Gateway Theatre of Shopping on Saturday, 25 April 2026. Already a glo...

item.title: Jason Saab continues play despite possible forward pass
item.description: During a recent NRL match, Jason Saab was allowed to continue running with the ball despite a controversial play that appeared to be a forward pass. The incident sparked discussions among fans and com...

item.title: ‘Beef’ Season 2 explores the struggles between generations and modern identity
item.description: The Netflix series 'Beef' is back for its second season, and its creator revealed that this new chapter will focus on generational conflicts and the lasting tensions between different age groups. Foll...

item.title: Elderly man rescued in Parañaque kidnapping as four suspects killed in police operation
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) successfully rescued a 78-year-old man who was kidnapped in Parañaque City on the evening of April 15, 2026. According to the PNP’s Anti-Kidnapping Group (AKG), th...

item.title: Windsor Firefighters Face Challenges Due to Severe Hoarding During House Fire
item.description: Firefighters in Windsor responded to a house fire that was significantly complicated by severe hoarding conditions. The accumulation of items throughout the home created obstacles that restricted acce...

item.title: UNESCO report shows Kenyan school heads spend more time on admin tasks than on improving learning
item.description: A new UNESCO Global Education Monitoring (GEM) Spotlight Report for Kenya 2025 has revealed that headteachers in the country are spending most of their time handling administrative duties instead of f...

item.title: UDA’s strategy to consolidate influence strains relations with coalition partners ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The United Democratic Alliance (UDA) is quietly pushing to consolidate its influence ahead of the 2027 elections, creating tensions within the Kenya Kwanza alliance. While UDA aims to form a dominant ...

item.title: Hunter Biden Accuses Trump Family of Hypocrisy Over Business Dealings
item.description: Hunter Biden has publicly criticised the Trump family's alleged profiteering from their business ventures while President Donald Trump remains in office. In an interview with Luke Radel for MeidasTouc...

item.title: UK Plans to Encourage Households to Shift Electricity Usage During Renewable Energy Surpluses
item.description: The UK government plans to ask households to increase their electricity consumption during periods of surplus renewable energy this summer. The initiative aims to balance the national power grid and r...

item.title: Declining Workforce in North America Faces Burnout and Low Job Optimism
item.description: Recent studies highlight growing concern over workforce morale in Canada and the U.S. Teams are shrinking due to economic uncertainty, political volatility, and automation, leaving employees overworke...

item.title: Judge Celebrates Daughter's Divorce with Festive Homecoming
item.description: Enyewe, this story is something else! A retired judge in India, Judge Gyanendra Kumar Sharma, threw a proper party – music, dancing, garlands, everything – to welcome his daughter, Pranita Vashistha, ...

item.title: Lenskart founder Peyush Bansal clarifies company stance on grooming and religious expression
item.description: Lenskart founder Peyush Bansal has recently addressed the concerns regarding the company's grooming policy, which had raised questions about religious freedom. Bansal assured that there are no restric...

item.title: Mercy Corps Holds First Poverty Graduation Ceremony in Tanzania for DREAMS Programme Beneficiaries in Tabora
item.description: Global humanitarian organisation Mercy Corps has successfully conducted its first in-country poverty graduation ceremony in Tanzania. The event took place on 14th and 15th April 2026 in Tabora Region,...

item.title: Tembisa police officers arrested for drugs at Boksburg Correctional Centre
item.description: Two officers from the Tembisa SAPS were apprehended for possessing illegal substances at the Boksburg Correctional Centre on 15 April. The arrest occurred around 09:30 during a routine vehicle inspect...

item.title: Seven Regions on Earth with Extremely Low or No Rainfall
item.description: Some places for this world no dey see rain at all, and some dey dry pass wetin you fit even imagine. Dis article list seven of such areas where rain dey extremely rare or no dey fall completely. For C...

item.title: Details of Meghan’s Sydney wellness retreat and ticket inclusions
item.description: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, is set to appear at a high-end wellness retreat in Coogee, Sydney, called 'Her Best Life', offering attendees a weekend focused on wellness and social experiences. The r...

item.title: CRA issues $5-million tax refund despite warning signs of fraudulent claim
item.description: The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) mistakenly issued a $4.96-million tax refund to a B.C. businesswoman, Teresa Wallace, despite indications that her return was fraudulent. Wallace's declared income and ...

item.title: Virginia Republican criticizes proposed congressional redistricting
item.description: Representative Ben Cline, a Republican from Virginia, criticized the Democratic-backed effort to redraw the state's congressional districts, claiming it would diminish rural voter influence. In an int...

item.title: LENTE and COMELEC Partner to Promote Inclusive Electoral Reforms
item.description: The VSO Corner, the official publication of the COMELEC Vulnerable Sectors Office, recently released Volume 2, Issue 2, highlighting its ongoing collaboration with LENTE to support inclusive electoral...

item.title: International student allowed to stay in Canada despite minor criminal involvement
item.description: An international student from India who came to Canada on a study permit in 2022 was permitted to remain in the country even after his student permit expired and he was involved in a failed attempt to...
